data.narrative:
Turion Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of 5 private companies at same or adjacent stage (Sierra Space, Cowboy Space Corporation, Varda Space Industries, Aalyria, Skyryse) and 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods produce quantifiable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $75M total raised as a valuation proxy, implying $142M. Peer-median anchoring across the 5 same-sector private comps yields $1.5B, with a range of $1.1B to $8.0B; the median reflects balanced representation of both Series B and Series C stage companies. Public-comp P/S analysis was not applicable; the Space & Aerospace sector median is 25.8x across the 6 public tickers, but Turion Space's revenue is not disclosed, precluding revenue multiple application.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $121M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $821M. The wide range reflects material stage and market positioning dispersion within the peer set. Key uncertainties include Turion's revenue trajectory relative to later-stage peers like Sierra Space ($8.0B), the sustainability of current Space & Aerospace valuations, and Turion's specific technology differentiation versus the broader cohort.
